title: Fix integer overflow in DimensionVector Rational pow
analysis: The Rational type in dimension.rs uses i8 for both num and den fields. In DimensionVector::pow() (line 176), the multiplication `self.0[i].num * nr.num` is i8 * i8 which silently wraps in release builds when the result exceeds i8::MAX (127). The same overflow risk exists in root() (line 166, den * n), Add (line 51-52, cross-multiplication), Sub (line 61-62), and Neg (line 71, -i8::MIN overflows). The fix is Option A from the task description: widen Rational's internal storage from i8 to i16. This is safe because the maximum product of two i8 values is 127*127=16,129 which fits comfortably in i16 (max 32,767). Dimension exponents in physics rarely exceed ~6, but the code must not silently corrupt on edge cases. The blast radius is contained: Rational fields (num/den) are only accessed within dimension.rs itself, with one external call site in reify-expr/src/lib.rs:315 that casts an i64 exponent to i8 for pow(). The content_hash() method serializes num/den as single bytes and must be updated to use 2 bytes each (i16). The as_i8() helper is defined but never called outside dimension.rs. No overflow-checks profile is set in Cargo.toml, confirming release builds use wrapping arithmetic by default.

steps.description: Write failing test `pow_overflow_does_not_silently_wrap` in dimension.rs: construct a DimensionVector with LENGTH exponent 64 (via basis_n(0, 64)), call .pow(2). Assert result exponent equals Rational { num: 128, den: 1 }. With current i8 arithmetic, 64*2=128 overflows i8::MAX (127) and wraps to -128 in release or panics in debug. This test must fail with current code.

Write failing test `root_overflow_does_not_silently_wrap` in dimension.rs: construct a DimensionVector with Rational exponent {1, 64} for LENGTH (via direct construction), call .root(3). Assert result has Rational { num: 1, den: 192 }. With current i8, den 64*3=192 overflows i8::MAX. Also write `rational_add_beyond_i8_range`: assert Rational::new(100, 1) + Rational::new(100, 1) == Rational::new(200, 1). With i8 Add, 100+100=200 overflows.

Write failing test `rational_neg_at_i8_boundary` in dimension.rs: assert -Rational { num: -128, den: 1 } produces Rational { num: 128, den: 1 }. In i8, negating -128 overflows (no positive 128 in i8). Write `rational_sub_beyond_i8_range`: assert Rational::new(50, 1) - Rational::new(-100, 1) == Rational::new(150, 1). The cross-multiplication 50*1 - (-100)*1 = 150 overflows i8.

Widen Rational from i8 to i16. All changes in dimension.rs: (1) Change struct fields num: i8 → i16, den: i8 → i16. (2) Change Rational::new signature from (i8, i8) to (i16, i16). (3) Change gcd function from (u8, u8) → u8 to (u16, u16) → u16, update unsigned_abs cast to u16. (4) Keep as_i8() with added range check (return None if value outside i8 range); no callers outside tests. (5) Update content_hash() from 18-byte buffer (9×2) to 36-byte buffer (9×4) using i16 to_le_bytes() for deterministic cross-platform serialization. (6) In pow(), cast the i8 parameter n to i16 when constructing the Rational: `Rational { num: n as i16, den: 1 }`. (7) In root(), cast the i8 parameter n to i16 in the den multiplication. (8) Update basis_n const fn parameter from i8 to i16. (9) Update AREA/VOLUME constants to use i16 literal. (10) In reify-expr/src/lib.rs:315, change `*n as i8` to `*n as i16` for the dimension.pow() call. All step-1/2/3 tests should now pass.

Write verification tests in dimension.rs: (1) `pow_large_exponent_round_trip` — LENGTH.pow(100).root(100) should equal LENGTH, verifying large exponents work end-to-end with i16. (2) `content_hash_determinism_with_wide_values` — verify content_hash is deterministic for a DimensionVector with exponents > 127. (3) `rational_display_wide_values` — verify Display formatting works for Rational values outside old i8 range (e.g., '200' not '-56').

Run full test suite: `cargo test -p reify-types -p reify-expr -p reify-stdlib`. All existing tests (rational_arithmetic, rational_normalization, dimension_mul, dimension_div, dimension_pow, force_dimension, root_area_to_length, root_length_4_to_length_2, root_length_to_fractional_exponent, root_dimensionless_stays_dimensionless, content_hash_determinism, and all reify-stdlib tests using DimensionVector) must pass. Fix any compilation errors or test failures caused by the type widening. Note: content_hash values will change due to wider serialization — if any test asserts specific hash values, update the expected values.

design_decisions.decision: Widen Rational from i8 to i16 (Option A from task description)
design_decisions.rationale: i16 completely eliminates overflow for all i8-range inputs since max(i8)*max(i8) = 127*127 = 16,129 < i16::MAX = 32,767. This is simpler than checked arithmetic (Option B) which would require error propagation through all dimension operations and changing method signatures. i32 is unnecessarily wide — dimension exponents are small numbers. i16 doubles storage from 2 to 4 bytes per Rational (18 to 36 bytes per DimensionVector) which is negligible.

design_decisions.decision: Keep pow() and root() accepting i8 parameters, widen internally
design_decisions.rationale: Physical dimension exponents never exceed ~6 and even exotic cases stay well within i8 range (±127). Keeping i8 parameters documents the intent that these are small integer exponents. The parameter is widened to i16 internally via `as i16` cast, so intermediate multiplication cannot overflow. This minimizes API surface changes while fixing the overflow.

design_decisions.decision: Change content_hash serialization from 18 to 36 bytes using to_le_bytes()
design_decisions.rationale: With i16 fields, each Rational needs 4 bytes (2 per field via to_le_bytes) instead of 2 bytes (1 per field via `as u8`). This changes hash values for all DimensionVectors. Since reify is v0.1 in active development with no persisted hash database, hash stability is not required. Using to_le_bytes() ensures deterministic cross-platform hashing and avoids the truncation bug of casting i16 to u8.

design_decisions.decision: Update reify-expr call site from `*n as i8` to `*n as i16`
design_decisions.rationale: The pow() call in eval_pow (reify-expr/src/lib.rs:315) casts an i64 exponent to i8, which truncates for values > 127 (e.g., 200 becomes -56). Widening to i16 reduces this risk. True range validation (returning Undef for exponents > i16::MAX) is out of scope for this task but would be a good follow-up.

reuse.how: Follow the same test structure: construct DimensionVector, perform operation, assert result equality or field-level Rational comparison. Reuse constants like DimensionVector::LENGTH, AREA, VOLUME.
Widen parameter and return types from u8 to u16. Algorithm is identical — just change the types. No logic changes needed.
Reuse to construct test fixtures with large exponents (e.g., basis_n(0, 64) for LENGTH^64). Widen parameter from i8 to i16.
Same hashing function, just with wider input buffer (36 bytes instead of 18). No changes to the hash module itself.
